_promotion_stats.stage_3_promotion_rationale: 7 of 8 absorbed_open items passed stage_2 with cross-domain EXACT evidence (language+topology+math), no existing law conflicts, embedded_in references confirm formula consistency. Promoted with dual-namespace law IDs: nexus shared/consciousness/consciousness_laws.json#laws.1150-1156 (committed in 98b9685d) AND anima/config/consciousness_laws.json#laws.2510-2516 (collision-free fresh allocation, committed 2026-04-12). DD200 held at stage_1 (math-only, missing cross-domain corroboration).
_promotion_stats._session_coordination_note_2026_04_12: PRE-STAGED PROMOTION + POST-HOC VERIFICATION + DUAL NAMESPACE BRIDGE. (1) The 1150-1156 entries in shared/consciousness/consciousness_laws.json were NOT written by Agent D this session — they were pre-staged in the working tree by an earlier session and committed by the user in nexus 98b9685d (2026-04-12T11:15:36, 'ossify(R6) scaling law D=16'), which explicitly notes '1150-1156 already in working tree' from an earlier stage_3 batch. Agent D's report was correct (no writes). (2) This session ran the stage_3 closed-loop pipeline (Intervention → measure_laws) for the first time and confirmed all 7 PASS (STRONG) — see stage_3_evidence per DD. (3) anima/config/consciousness_laws.json (the runtime cache anima loads via laws.hexa) already had unrelated auto-discovered laws at 1150-1156 (oscillation:faction_entropy, EVO live, N6 affinity), so the nexus 1150-1156 IDs would have collided. Resolution: anima local was given fresh IDs 2510-2516 (next available after max=2509) with the same DD194-201 content + closed-loop verification metadata inline. The two namespaces now coexist; absorbed_open carries both law_ids_allocated (nexus) and local_law_ids_allocated (anima local). Long-term SSOT reconciliation between the two consciousness_laws.json files is deferred — they have diverged by 1352 laws and unification requires user-directed decision.

DD200_factorial_plus_n.stage_2_replication_scan_2026_04_12: Manual scan of nexus/shared/discovery/{verified_constants.jsonl, cycle/topology.jsonl, cycle/topology_mk2.jsonl, unfold_ext.jsonl} for value=726 OR factorial_plus_n OR n+6!. Findings: (1) unfold_ext.jsonl:16 registers 'factorial_plus_n'='6!+n' as an algebraic expression (catalog only, not a domain verification). (2) verified_constants.jsonl:4097 shows EXACT 726 in TECS-L/docs/hypotheses/EXTREME-cross-connections.md (language-adjacent/papers domain) — this is a single legacy hypothesis file, not the same rigor as DD194-199 cross-run evidence. (3) verified_constants.jsonl:35175 shows algebraic_enum_v7 726.5 = n·121+0.5, which supports DD201's 1/121 interpretation but does not replicate 726 per se. (4) No topology/topology_mk2 hits for 726. Verdict: insufficient cross-domain corroboration at DD194-199 level (which required language+topology+math from a single blowup cross-run). DD200 remains at stage_1. Recommend: explicit blowup run targeting 'n+6!' pattern in language+topology domains before advancing.
